instruction: Want a quick summary of exceptions versus standard rules?
output:
## Want A Quick Summary of Exceptions Versus Standard Rules Policy
NON: DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA) This Non: Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of 17* July 2025 (the "Effective Date") by and between: Beyond: Prowise Ltd, a company incorporated under the laws of [Insert Jurisdiction], with its principal place of business at Miawl, Bracknell Rd, RG42 6JX ("Disclosing Party" or "Company"); and Joe: Bloggs, a company/individual located at Drury Lane, England ("Receiving Party"). Together: referred to as the "Parties". 1.: PURPOSE The Parties wish to explore a potential business relationship (the “Purpose”), during which each Party may disclose or receive certain confidential, proprietary, or non: public information. 2.: DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Confidential: Information means any information disclosed by either Party, in any form (oral, written, electronic, or otherwise), that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential, including but not limited to: Business plans, commercial data, technical specifications: Software, source code, algorithms, models, processes Financial: records, customer and supplier information Contractual: terms, pricing, and employee data 3.: OBLIGATIONS OF THE RECEIVING PARTY The Receiving Party agrees to: Maintain all Confidential Information in strict confidence: Use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose - Not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties without prior written consent Protect: Confidential Information with at least the same level of care it uses to protect its own information 4.: EXCLUSIONS This Agreement does not apply to information that: Was lawfully known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure: Becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party Is: independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information Is: disclosed under a legal obligation or court order (provided prompt notice is given to the Disclosing Party) 5.: TERM AND TERMINATION This: Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of [3] years from the Effective Date. Either Party may terminate this Agreement upon 30 days’ written notice. The obligations of confidentiality shall survive termination for a period of [5] years. 6.: RETURN OR DESTRUCTION Upon: termination of this Agreement or upon request, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information and confirm such return or destruction in writing. 7.: NO LICENSE OR WARRANTY Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any license or right to use the Confidential Information, or any patent, copyright, or other intellectual property rights. 8.: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION This: Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [Insert Governing Law], and the Parties agree to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of [Insert Jurisdiction]. 9.: GENERAL This: Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter. Any: amendments must be in writing and signed by both Parties. If: any provision of this Agreement is held to be unenforceable, the remainder shall remain in full force. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Non: Disclosure Agreement as of the Effective Date. DISCLOSING: PARTY Name: Title: Signature: _ Date: RECEIVING: PARTY Name: Title: Signature: _ Date:.

What You Must Do
- Submit only eligible, well-documented expenses so they are accepted.
- The obligations of confidentiality shall survive termination for a period of [5] years.
- 6.: RETURN OR DESTRUCTION Upon: termination of this Agreement or upon request, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information and confirm such return or destruction in writing.

What's Prohibited
- Expect non-claimable expenses to be declined, logged, and escalated if repeated.
- Treat anything not explicitly allowed in the policy as prohibited.

Spending Limits
- Stay within the £100 limit unless you have written approval.
- Keep client entertainment within the per-person threshold stated in the policy.
- APO must be raised for all third: party purchases above the threshold outlined in the Delegation of Authority policy.

Approval Requirements
- Capture manager or Finance approval before using the card for unusual spend.
- Record pre-approval details with the expense submission.
- PRINCIPLES: All expenses must be business-related, reasonable, and pre-authorised where applicable.

Examples
- Example: n-claimable expenses include alcohol (unless pre-approved), personal travel upgrades, and hotel incidentals.
- Example: NON: DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA) This Non: Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of 17* July 2025 (the "Effective Date") by and between: Beyond: Prowise Ltd, a company incorporated under the laws of [Insert Jurisdiction], with its principal place of business at Miawl, Bracknell Rd, RG42 6JX ("Disclosing Party" or "Company"); and Joe: Bloggs, a company/individual located at Drury Lane, England ("Receiving Party").
- Example: 8.: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION This: Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [Insert Governing Law], and the Parties agree to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of [Insert Jurisdiction].

Exceptions
- Exception: Allowed when pre-approved), personal travel upgrades, and hotel incidentals.
- Exception: if pre-approved and under the £100 per person threshold for client meetings.
- Exception: Allowed when consumed in approved business entertainment Personal travel or upgrades (e.g., Business/First Class without pre: approval) - Fines (traffic, parking, penalties) Non: business-related expenses (e.g., clothing, gifts, toiletries, subscriptions) Hotel incidentals (e.g., mini: bar, movies, spa, personal calls) Family/companion travel, accommodation, or meals: Donations or sponsorships not authorised through Corporate Affairs 5.: SUBMISSION AND APPROVAL REQUIREMENTS All: expense claims must be submitted within 30 calendar days of the transaction.

instruction: Would you like me to surface the related purchase order or contract details?
output:
Context

I checked both our procurement records and policy library but couldn't find anything specific about Would you like me to surface the related purchase order or contract details. To keep things moving, please log the details in ProcWise or reach out to the procurement operations team so we can capture the right guidance.

Context

I've reviewed the reference notes on Would you like me to surface the related purchase order or contract details. Here's the quick take-away: Your category manager is the first point of contact, with the Procurement Helpdesk covering transactional issues.

Findings

Here's the context that frames this question: Your category manager is the first point of contact, with the Procurement Helpdesk covering transactional issues.

Implications

Use these points to steer the next conversation, and just shout if you want deeper analysis on any thread.

Overview

To help you weigh that's better, here's the headline insight I found: Policy insight: DELEGATION: OF AUTHORITY POLICY

Document Owner: Chief Procurement Officer

Approval Authority: Executive Leadership Team

Policy Number: 7003947

Effective Date: 14% July 2025

Review Cycle: Annual or as required

Scope: All Group Entities, Business Units, and Corporate Functions

1.: POLICY STATEMENT

This: Delegation of Authority (DoA) Policy establishes a clear and consistent governance framework for approving financial commitments, contractual obligations, and strategic decisions across the Group.

Side-by-Side Comparison

Here's how the options line up:

| Aspect | Policy | DELEGATION | OF AUTHORITY POLICY

Document Owner |

|--------|--------|--------|--------|

| Spend | $10,000 | N/A | N/A |

Analysis

What to take from this:

**Policy**

  • Strengths:
  • Policy insight: DELEGATION: OF AUTHORITY POLICY

Document Owner: Chief Procurement Officer

Approval Authority: Executive Leadership Team

Policy Number: 7003947

Effective Date: 14% July 2025

Review Cycle: Annual or as required

Scope: All Group Entities, Business Units, and Corporate Functions

1.: POLICY STATEMENT

This: Delegation of Authority (DoA) Policy establishes a clear and consistent governance framework for approving financial commitments, contractual obligations, and strategic decisions across the Group.

  • Policy insight: DELEGATION: OF AUTHORITY POLICY

Document Owner: Chief Procurement Officer

Approval Authority: Executive Leadership Team

Policy Number: 7003947

Effective Date: 14% July 2025

Review Cycle: Annual or as required

Scope: All Group Entities, Business Units, and Corporate Functions

1.: POLICY STATEMENT

This: Delegation of Authority (DoA) Policy establishes a clear and consistent governance framework for approving financial commitments, contractual obligations, and strategic decisions across the Group.

  • GOVERNING PRINCIPLES: No individual may approve a transaction from which they derive direct personal benefit

Delegation is by position, not by individual, and is non: transferable unless formally documented

All: financial and contractual approvals must align with Group strategy, budget, and legal requirements

All: decisions must be documented and supported by the appropriate system of record (e.g., PO, contract, requisition)

Breaches: of this policy will be subject to investigation and appropriate disciplinary measures

4.: APPLICABILITY This policy applies to:

All Group employees, including permanent, temporary, and interim staff: Authorised external parties acting on behalf of the Group (e.g. contractors, consultants)

All financial commitments, including but not limited to: operating and capital expenditure, third-party contracts, leases, consultancy engagements, and sponsorships

6.: DELEGATION RULES AND CONTROLS

Delegation: limits must be respected at all times and are not cumulative or bypassable

Temporary delegation (e.g., during extended absence) must be pre: approved by the next- level authority and recorded via the Delegation Register

Allcommitments: must be supported by validated documentation (e.g. business case, budget approval, contract, PO)

System: role permissions must reflect authorised delegation and be reviewed periodically by Compliance or Internal Audit

7.: COMPLIANCE ASSURANCE

Delegation: compliance is subject to regular internal audit, financial controls testing, and spot checks

All: exceptions must be logged in the Delegation Exception Register and approved by the CFO or designated governance officer

Non: compliance may result in loss of delegation privileges and formal disciplinary proceedings in line with the Group Code of Conduct

8.: SUPPORT, CLARIFICATION & ESCALATION

Policy Advice & Interpretation: Procurement Governance Lead or Group Financial Controller

Delegation Setup / System Roles: Enterprise Systems or HR Operations

Delegation: Directory & Tools:

5.: DELEGATED APPROVAL MATRIX

Transaction: Type

Approval: Threshold (USD)

Minimum: Authorisation Level

Operational: & Capital Expenditure (POs)

<: $10,000

Department: Manager

$10,001: $50,000

Senior: Manager / Category Lead

$50,001: $250,000

Director: / Function Head

$250,001: $1,000,000

Business: Unit Lead / VP

> $1,000,000 CFO + CPO + CEO (if strategic) Contracts (new, renewals, < $100,000 Legal + Procurement amendments) Manager > $100,000 Legal + CPO + Business Sponsor Consultancy Engagements < $50,000 Line Manager + Procurement Advisor > $50,000 VP + Legal + Procurement Lead Budget Endorsement < $500,000 Finance Controller + (Annual or Project: Based) Functional Director > $500,000 CFO + CEO
